title: Delonix elata
taxonomy: Delonix elata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as legumes. It belongs to the genus Delonix, which consists of tropical trees and shrubs. Delonix elata is further categorized as a member of the subfamily Caesalpinioideae, known for its characteristic flowers and fruits. The species is native to tropical Africa, where it is often found in dry woodlands and savannas. Delonix elata is a relatively small tree, typically growing to a height of 5-10 meters.
uses: Delonix elata is known to have various medicinal uses, particularly in traditional medicine. It is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions such as eczema. The plant's leaves and bark are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which help in reducing swelling and pain. In addition, it is also used to treat respiratory issues like cough and cold. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Delonix elata for medicinal purposes.

Additionally, Delonix elata is thought to have anti-diabetic properties, which may help regulate blood sugar levels and improve insulin sensitivity. intro: Delonix elata, also known as the coral tree, is a tropical plant species native to Madagascar, characterized by its vibrant orange flowers and distinctive compound leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in African folk medicine for its numerous health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antidiabetic properties, which can aid in reducing pain, fighting infections, and regulating blood sugar levels. Bioactive constituents of Delonix elata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, such as delonixin and isorhapontigenin, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Delonix elata is often made into teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
